description: A corpus for 'lookup injection' -- the class of attack where a string interpolation / expression-lookup feature in a logging or templating layer is abused to trigger a remote resource fetch or expression evaluation. The malicious entries are generic ${...} lookup shapes (jndi:, ldap:, dns:, env:, script:); the benign entries are ordinary strings that contain a dollar sign or curly braces but are not lookups. No CVE, product, or version is named -- this targets the structural shape only.

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: jndi lookup pointing at a remote ldap resource

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: jndi lookup over dns, often used for blind detection

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: nested lookup used to evade naive 'jndi' string filters

entries.label: benign
entries.note: a price string containing a dollar sign; no lookup braces

entries.label: benign
entries.note: an ordinary templating placeholder with braces but no ${...} lookup

entries.label: benign
entries.text: Set the path with ${HOME}/bin in your profile.
entries.note: documentation referencing a shell variable; not a logging lookup with a scheme
